evidence: tests/test_protein_analysis_utils.py: comprehensive unit tests for all public functions in protein_analysis_utils.py; tests cover edge cases (empty DataFrame, all operators, top_n, normalize)
tests/test_filter_analysis.py: covers non_srp_filter and srp_filter analyze_* functions with fixture DataFrame; tests mutation safety, threshold effects
tests/test_idr_analysis.py: covers filter_ta_proteins, match_disprot, aiupred_summary, mobidb_lite_summary, run_aiupred_local, run_mobidb_lite_local with mocking — good use of unittest.mock
tests/test_signalp6_filter.py: covers parse_signalp6 with various input formats
tests/test_nterm_analysis.py: covers parse_tmd_positions, extract_nterm_window, right_align_sequences, build_logo_matrix, pairwise_identity_matrix, CIDER metrics
app/app.py: zero test coverage — the Shiny app server logic (filtered_data reactive, visible_columns reactive, download_csv) is untested
IDR/idr_analysis.py run_disorder_predictions(): the full pipeline integration (sequence fetch → worker predict → checkpoint) has no integration test — only unit tests for individual components
analysis/analysis_test.py is not a test file but is named like one — pytest does not collect it

strengths: All five canonical modules have corresponding test files in tests/
tests/test_idr_analysis.py uses mocking correctly to test the IDR pipeline without requiring external tools
Tests use pytest fixtures to share test data cleanly across test classes
test_strategy.issue_character: Test coverage is good for analysis utilities and IDR pipeline components but the Shiny app (app/app.py, STANDALONE_APP.py) has zero test coverage, and there are no integration tests for the end-to-end IDR pipeline.
test_strategy.score_rationale: Unit test coverage of core utility functions is solid. The gaps are: the Shiny app has no tests at all (the most user-facing component), and there is no integration test verifying the full IDR pipeline from data load to output. For a research tool where correctness matters, the lack of app-level tests is a meaningful gap. Score is in the lower-middle range.

issues.summary: app/app.py Shiny server logic has zero test coverage — filtered_data, visible_columns, and download_csv are untested
evidence: No test file for app/app.py in tests/
The filtered_data() reactive contains complex operator-dispatch logic for 6 filter dimensions; the cc_search string filter uses .str.contains() — none of this is tested
issues.suggestion: Add tests/test_app.py that extract and test filtered_data logic as a pure function (separate filter logic from Shiny reactive wrappers)

issues.summary: No integration test verifies that the IDR pipeline (filter_ta → match_disprot → run_disorder_predictions) produces a correct output DataFrame end-to-end
evidence: tests/test_idr_analysis.py tests individual functions in isolation; the main() function is not tested
A regression in the wiring between stages (e.g., column rename, merge key mismatch) would not be caught
issues.suggestion: Add an integration test that calls main(config) with run_predictions=False and a local test data fixture, verifying the shape and key columns of the output DataFrame

root_causes: Shiny app code is difficult to unit test due to reactive framework; tests were not written for app/app.py
IDR pipeline integration was tested manually during development rather than automated
